# Stream retention

> **Feature delivered — acceptance criteria.** Generated from the as-built BDD tests in
> [`test/stories/stream_retention_story_test.exs`](../../../echo/apps/echo_mq/test/stories/stream_retention_story_test.exs). Every scenario below is a passing
> ExUnit test driving the real `EchoMQ` surface against Valkey, so the catalogue cannot
> drift from the code.

**4 scenarios.**

## Scenario: a MAXLEN trim keeps the newest entries and removes the older, never deleting inside the window

- **Given** a stream flooded with six EVT records appended in mint order
- **When** trim/4 keeps the two newest with an EXACT MAXLEN window
- **Then** exactly the four oldest are removed and the two newest SURVIVE a read-back (the bound held)

## Scenario: a MINID trim removes entries minted before a mint instant and keeps those at or after it

- **Given** a stream with three records minted below a horizon and three at or above it (ascending)
- **When** trim/4 trims by MINID(horizon), the floor derived from Snowflake.min_for/1
- **Then** the below-instant records are GONE and the at/above-instant records SURVIVE (the half-open [dt, ∞) edge)

## Scenario: the blast radius is bounded by the declared window — an in-window entry survives a trim

- **Given** a stream flooded with eight records and one in-window entry tracked by its receipt
- **When** trim/4 keeps the newest three (the tracked newest is inside the window)
- **Then** the tracked in-window entry still reads back — over-deletion would be a LOUD failure

## Scenario: retention is a property of the stream — the opt-in driver trims with no consumer present

- **Given** a flooded stream and a declared per-stream policy held BEAM-side (no keyspace subkey)
- **When** a named, owner-started trim driver sweeps the declared policy (NO StreamConsumer running)
- **Then** the stream is trimmed to its window even though nothing drains it (retention decoupled from liveness)
